model_description: Compliant DLP-grokking model: a shared Transformer residue-encoder maps (a mod p, p) and (b mod p, p) to vectors that are combined by ADDITION (a log-space / discrete-log inductive bias), then a decoder emits the answer digits. No precomputed discrete-log table or hand-coded modular arithmetic; the field multiplication is learned. Targets small primes (tiers 1-3); falls back to 0 for p >= 10^5.
training_description: Trained from scratch on synthetic (a mod p, b mod p, p) -> (a*b mod p) examples sampled across many small primes (random primes spanning the tier 1-3 bit ranges), so the network must generalise across primes rather than memorise one. Cross-entropy on fixed-width MSB-first answer digits; Adam on Apple MPS.
